items.title: Harbinger Low Intensity Power 30.5k/32.5k
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the two-button version, all you want to do is spam dagger two right after you exit shroud or right before you enter shroud. In shroud, spam skill two, Dark Barrage, and otherwise autoattack. Shroud is ranged, so you do not have to be in melee while you are in shroud, but you do want to be in melee when you exit, so I recommend staying in melee range.

There is a small optimization with this build. If you aim for around four casts of shroud skill two, then exit shroud a little early, you can squeeze out a tiny bit more damage. If you just camp shroud until it runs out, use dagger two after it ends, and use dagger two before going back in without tracking Soul Barbs, you will lose maybe 500 DPS. It is not a big deal, so feel free to relax if you want.

For the four-button version, play it the same way as the two-button version, but add the two wells. Your opener is the two wells, dagger two, then shroud, and then you spam shroud skill two. The optimization is to use about four Dark Barrages in shroud, because Soul Barbs gives you 10% more damage for about 15 seconds after entering or exiting shroud. Try to maximize that. With the nerfs, we cannot be quite as brain-dead anymore, so camp about four Dark Barrages, exit shroud, use dagger two, use the two wells, and right before you enter shroud you can use another dagger two.

If you want to min-max the four-button build, you can use an offhand sword and add sword four and five. Use them similarly to the wells: use the wells, use the sword skills, use dagger two. That probably gives another 500 DPS or so. The main thing is still to exit shroud after about four Dark Barrages.

For the overall build rundown, this build runs dagger/warhorn. Warhorn is not required, but I included it so people have a little CC. Warhorn four is CC, so use it if you see a breakbar. Dagger is used because the build mostly camps shroud, and dagger autoattacks generate life force. That makes it easy to regain life force, re-enter shroud, and camp shroud again. Dagger two is decent damage. Dagger three is a boon strip, but it is not useful for the basic rotation, so only use it if you want to strip boons.

In Harbinger Shroud, skill four is CC when you are above the blight threshold, which you pretty much always will be, and skill five is also CC. If you are in shroud and see a breakbar, use four and five to help break it.

Trait-wise, there is not much complicated going on. The build takes damage traits. It gets increased damage when the target is below 50% from Close to Death and Relic of the Eagle, so your DPS starts lower and increases a lot during the last half of the fight. Soul Barbs matters more now. Previously the build could ignore it more because power creep was high, and I simplified the build even though it lost a little damage. You can still camp shroud until it runs out if you want, then use dagger two and your wells. Depending on which version you play, that may be a 500 to 1,000 DPS loss. If you want to min-max, exit shroud after about four Dark Barrages.

Implacable Foe gives stability and damage reduction when entering Harbinger Shroud, so you may be able to facetank something right after entering shroud because of the 50% damage reduction. Cascading Corruption means you want to stand next to your target because you have an aura that deals damage. You should be doing that anyway.

For utilities, you can run pretty much whatever heal skill and elite skill you want. I originally had elixirs, but they are slightly worse DPS than running minions, so I slotted in the minions. Any heal skill, including the elixir, is fine. For elite, you should probably run Summon Flesh Golem because you want the CC, but you can run other elites if you want.

If you are playing the four-button build, you run the wells. You can replace one well with useful necromancer utility. Corrosive Poison Cloud is a projectile destruction skill with a long duration and a fairly short cooldown, especially with alacrity. It is very strong when you need projectile destruction, such as on Sabir if your supports are not already bringing projectile destruction or reflects. Spectral Grasp is a strong CC skill, life force skill, pull skill, and has very long range. Summon Flesh Wurm has niche uses. Well of Power can cleanse conditions on allies and can be decent if you are struggling with conditions. If you swap something, swap one of the wells, or one of the minions if you are playing the two-button build.

To play this build as quickness, you need to change a few things. Take Deathly Haste instead of Cascading Corruption. Deathly Haste passively gives quickness and fury while you are in Harbinger Shroud. I heavily recommend Twisted Medicine because it gives concentration, which is boon duration, and makes your elixirs grant their boons to allies within the impact radius. I also recommend Elixir of Anguish. It gives swiftness and quickness, and with Twisted Medicine it gives that quickness to allies too.

Those are the minimum adjustments: Deathly Haste, Twisted Medicine, and Elixir of Anguish. After that, you can mostly play the build as is. You can camp shroud and accept a little less damage by ignoring Soul Barbs, or you can do the four Dark Barrages, exit, and use Elixir of Anguish. As long as you are using Elixir of Anguish and shroud, and people are standing near you or you are standing near them, you will give quickness.

If you struggle to maintain quickness, slot in more boon duration. Diviner's gear gives power, precision, ferocity, and concentration, but it has less precision than Berserker's, so make sure you still crit cap if you use it. You may need some Assassin's pieces. You can also swap Sigil of Force to Superior Sigil of Concentration for 10% boon duration. Superior Rune of Fireworks is another option if you need more boon duration, though it costs some damage. The bare minimum setup should be enough if played well, but if you still need more quickness uptime, add boon duration.
